customer_anxiety_profile: Cost-anxious but genuinely interested. Donna raised price concerns twice and pre-armed a DQ condition ('if this is gonna be very expensive, you could just take me off the list') — but she also elaborated unprompted about her prior solar research and stayed warm through the call. Her anxiety is about affordability, not skepticism about solar itself. She has not been flipped off the cost frame.
unresolved_concerns: Cost anxiety: Donna stated twice that she remembers solar being very expensive. Holland reassured generically but never flipped the $300/month bill into an alignment-against-the-utility argument. The closer should expect this concern to resurface and should be prepared to anchor to the bill figure.

No locked callback time: the Monday follow-up is a soft mutual agreement, not a scheduled appointment. Donna may or may not call. Holland self-disclosed as a potentially unreliable follow-up. The closer or a follow-up system should treat this as a warm lead requiring active outreach Monday.
Shading variable unresolved: tall trees on the southern exposure were confirmed but not probed further. Canopy density and any tree-removal plans are unknown. The designer needs this flagged.
sensitive_topics_raised: Cost anxiety raised twice — Donna pre-armed a DQ condition on price. Do not open with product features or system size; open with the bill and the savings frame.

Long utility relationship (Mora San Miguel since parents' era) — Donna has no online account and receives mail-only bills. She is not tech-forward. Keep the closer interaction simple and low-friction.
exact_promises_rep_made: Holland promised to send a text with contact info (05:23: 'I'll just send you a text').
Holland implied a Monday callback but framed it as Donna's responsibility to initiate (06:01: 'just give me a call').
Holland promised to pull up the bill and make a copy once Donna sends the photo (06:07: 'I'll try and I'll pull that up, and I'll see if I can make a copy of it for you').
closer_should_know: Donna's cost anxiety was never resolved — it was papered over with generic reassurance. The $300/month bill is the lever. Walk in with that number as the villain, not solar as the solution. She is warm and cooperative but has a standing DQ condition on price that will resurface the moment the proposal feels expensive.
